h2h_summary: Belgium and Egypt have met twice in friendlies, splitting wins 1-1, with Belgium winning 3-0 in 2018 and Egypt winning 2-1 in 2022.
team_style_home: Belgium play an attacking 4-2-3-1 built on possession, quick transitions and wide pace through Doku, with De Bruyne orchestrating from deep and Lukaku as the target striker.
team_style_away: Egypt set up in a compact, disciplined 4-2-3-1 that prioritizes defensive organization and concedes minimal space before releasing Salah and Marmoush on the counter-attack.
wc_history_home: Belgium have appeared in 14 World Cups, with their finest hour coming in 2018 when they finished third — their best-ever result — defeating Brazil in the quarter-finals.
wc_history_away: Egypt have appeared at only three previous World Cups (1934, 1990, 2018) and have never won a match or progressed past the group stage in any of those tournaments.
group_context: Group G also contains Iran and New Zealand; Belgium are strong favourites to top the group, while Egypt — led by Mohamed Salah in what is likely his final World Cup — are battling Iran for the crucial second qualification spot.
strengths_home: World-class creative talent in De Bruyne and explosive width from Doku
Elite goalkeeper in Courtois providing a solid last line of defence
Prolific attack — 29 goals in 8 qualifying matches — with depth across European top clubs
strengths_away: Mohamed Salah and Omar Marmoush form one of the tournament's most dangerous attacking duos
Exceptional defensive record in CAF qualifying — seven clean sheets, only two goals conceded in ten matches
Strong domestic spine from Al Ahly combined with European-based quality gives tactical flexibility
